prose:
Frontier-orbital gap of benzene — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RIJK density-fitted) with PySCF  (gas phase), charge 0, multiplicity 1.  Geometry: fixed at the user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz coordinates (no re-optimization; single-point orbitals).  SCF converged in 8 cycles.

Results (all in eV)
• HOMO energy  = −7.0468329
• LUMO energy  = −0.41574728
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857

No warnings were reported by the engine.  The calculation ran with integrity.status = "ok" (integrity.trustworthy = true).

Live PySCF output was streamed to the standard chemkit frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory; see that file for full SCF and orbital details.

Method provenance block
  Property: frontier-orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap
  Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RIJK density fitting)
  Geometry: user-supplied benzene.xyz; no relaxation (single-point)
  Solvent model: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
  Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell)
  Software: PySCF (via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
  Convergence: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings

These values are purely computational (provenance = computed).
